category_title: Sixth-grade students with diverse learning and emotional disorders
minimal_context: The student is asked to read a story from the required curriculum book in their sixth-grade English class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your book and start reading the story from our required curriculum.

STUDENT: I don't have any supplies for the first day of school. Besides, this is boring and I'm worried that I won't be able to focus on reading a book.

TUTOR: Here is a borrowed pencil. Please just try your best to read.

STUDENT: (Puts head on the desk) I can't do it.

TUTOR: Please sit up and keep trying to read the story.

STUDENT: (Falls out of their seat, then gets up to sharpen the borrowed pencil for the fourth time in 10 minutes, 'accidentally' hitting another student on the way)

options.A: Read and discuss the unfolding plot elements of the story with the student.
options.B: Joke a lot with the student and laugh at oneself during the reading task.
options.C: Tell the student, "Don't say you can't, you can," regarding the reading task.
options.D: Follow the student's lead and maintain a physical distance during the reading task.
